mission: Track speculative Top-K optimization ideas as testable hypotheses with exactness class, k regime, likely failure mode, and first benchmark gate.
evidence_policy: A timing record must include idea_id, k_regime, exactness class, repair policy, and same-hardware baselines.
Contract-changing ideas are not Top-K wins unless the caller explicitly accepts the changed operation.
Negative examples should be kept when an idea is correct but loses.

ideas.name: Multi-pivot fence
ideas.core_hypothesis: Count several thresholds per pass, then repair the bucket containing the kth score.
ideas.first_experiment: Four-pivot FP32 pass for k=64,128,256,512 against hipCUB DeviceTopK and DeviceRadixSort.

ideas.name: Exponent-first radix
ideas.core_hypothesis: Use high-order sortable float-key bins to isolate likely winners before mantissa-level repair.
ideas.first_experiment: Histogram exponent/sign bins, compact the winning bin, and sort/repair candidates.
failure_modes: most values land in one bucket

ideas.name: Complement Top-K
ideas.core_hypothesis: When k/n is high, select or sort the bottom n-k and derive the top-k complement.
ideas.first_experiment: Sweep k/n=0.1,0.25,0.5,0.9 against full radix sort.
failure_modes: sorted output still needs large sort

ideas.name: Unsorted-first late-sort
ideas.core_hypothesis: Return or consume unsorted candidates and sort only when the caller requires sorted output.
ideas.first_experiment: Compare unsorted membership, unsorted plus k-sort, and always-sorted paths for k=8,16,32,64.
failure_modes: hipCUB unsorted baseline is already stronger

ideas.name: FlashTopK epilogue
ideas.core_hypothesis: Do not materialize logits; have LM-head GEMM tiles emit local Top-K candidates for a hierarchical merge.
ideas.first_experiment: Composable Kernel epilogue sketch that writes per-tile candidates and compares against materialized GEMM plus Top-K.

ideas.name: Temporal candidate cache
ideas.core_hypothesis: Reuse prior decode step candidate blocks, pivots, or thresholds, then validate exactly.
ideas.first_experiment: Record hint hit rate and repair rate over real decode traces.
failure_modes: repair requires full scan every time

ideas.name: Hot-block vocab layout
ideas.core_hypothesis: Model-specific vocab permutation can make high-probability blocks easier to find and merge.
ideas.first_experiment: Offline vocab permutation plus inverse index repair on one fixed model.
failure_modes: weight layout integration is too costly
cold blocks still need full scan

ideas.name: K-regime JIT ladder
ideas.core_hypothesis: Compile or cache specialized kernels for common k values instead of using one generic kernel.
ideas.first_experiment: hipRTC or static dispatch for k=1,2,4,8,16,32,64,128,256 with amdclang++ resource/ISA resource capture.
failure_modes: code size and compile time grow
dynamic request k values defeat caching

ideas.name: Boundary-tie microkernel
ideas.core_hypothesis: Run a fast deterministic path for rows without kth-boundary duplicates and repair rare tied rows.
ideas.first_experiment: Quantized, all-equal, and random FP32 tie-rate sweep.
failure_modes: fast path accidentally violates deterministic order

ideas.name: Ragged length buckets
ideas.core_hypothesis: Bucket segments by length and route them to warp, CTA, or multi-CTA kernels.
ideas.first_experiment: Zipf segment-length benchmark versus padded PyTorch and segmented hipCUB-style pipeline.
failure_modes: empty and short segments expose output-policy bugs

ideas.name: Score-bin bitset select
ideas.core_hypothesis: Use coarse score bins and warp ballots to emit threshold candidates with compact bitsets.
ideas.first_experiment: Per-tile bin counters plus bitset candidate emit for k=16,32,64,128.
failure_modes: bin collisions create huge repair set

ideas.name: Two-pass Top-K plus CDF
ideas.core_hypothesis: For Top-K sampling, find the kth threshold, then compute exp sum and sample without sorting/materializing the k-list.
ideas.first_experiment: Compare against block-topk-sampling and FlashInfer for k=4,8,16,32.
failure_modes: two passes lose for tiny k

ideas.name: Confidence-gated argmax
ideas.core_hypothesis: Very peaked rows can skip broad Top-K work under greedy or approximate sampling policies.
ideas.first_experiment: Measure quality/perplexity and latency with margin thresholds; never classify as exact Top-K.
failure_modes: quality loss hides in rare tokens

ideas.name: Grouped tensor-parallel merge
ideas.core_hypothesis: Each vocab shard emits local top-k candidates, then ranks exchange only candidate lists.
ideas.first_experiment: RCCL allgather of candidate pairs plus deterministic merge for tensor-parallel vocab shards.
failure_modes: local candidate count insufficient for ties

ideas.name: Adversarial autotuner
ideas.core_hypothesis: Generate cases that make each candidate fail before trusting random-logit speedups.
ideas.first_experiment: Add all-equal, kth-boundary ties, high k/n, NaNs, flat logits, and ragged extremes to the harness.

ideas.name: Low-precision prefilter
ideas.core_hypothesis: Use half/int8/fp8 shadow scores or approximate loads to prune obvious losers, then reload exact scores for repair.
ideas.first_experiment: Keep top-M approximate candidates for k=16,64,256, then prove or repair against exact FP32.
failure_modes: close boundary scores force large repair sets
shadow buffer maintenance costs too much

ideas.name: Block upper-bound pruning
ideas.core_hypothesis: Use certified per-vocab-block upper bounds to skip blocks whose maximum possible score cannot beat the current kth score.
ideas.first_experiment: Compare bound-based live-block scans against full scans on materialized logits and LM-head-integrated traces.
failure_modes: bound computation costs as much as logits

ideas.name: Max-K superset cache
ideas.core_hypothesis: For rows or requests with multiple requested k values, compute Kmax once and slice smaller k outputs.
ideas.first_experiment: Group rows by Kmax and compare against separate k-specialized kernels for mixed k=4,8,16,32,64 batches.
failure_modes: Kmax much larger than most rows need
sorted Kmax overpays for sample-only rows

ideas.name: Policy bucket scheduler
ideas.core_hypothesis: Group rows by k, sortedness, dtype, output mode, and sampling policy before launching specialized kernels.
ideas.first_experiment: Measure bucket overhead, launch count, graph replay compatibility, and latency versus one generic mixed-policy kernel.

ideas.name: Deterministic packed key
ideas.core_hypothesis: Pack sortable score bits and token-id tie policy into one integer key to simplify deterministic compare/sort/select.
ideas.first_experiment: Build 64-bit packed-key oracle for FP32 logits and compare radix-sort overhead against pair compares.
failure_modes: score and token bits do not both fit cleanly

ideas.name: In-warp reservoir duel
ideas.core_hypothesis: Let lanes hold tiny reservoirs and duel pairwise with shuffle exchange instead of maintaining sorted per-thread lists.
ideas.first_experiment: Compare reservoir duels against insertion lists and bitonic networks for k=4,8,16,32.
failure_modes: duel schedule misses candidates without repair
insertion list remains simpler and faster

ideas.name: Loser-tree block merge
ideas.core_hypothesis: Use a tournament or loser tree to merge sorted block candidate lists instead of repeated full k-list insertion.
ideas.first_experiment: Second-pass-only benchmark with synthetic sorted lists, then real first-pass candidate lists for k=64,128,256.
failure_modes: first-pass lists are not sorted cheaply

ideas.name: Persistent Top-K service
ideas.core_hypothesis: Use resident CTAs and a request queue to keep policy metadata and workspace hot for small decode batches.
ideas.first_experiment: Compare ordinary launch, HIP Graph replay, and persistent service on the same batch-1..16 request trace.

ideas.name: Cluster DGFX candidate ring
ideas.core_hypothesis: Use CDNA3/CDNA4/RDNA4 cluster shared memory to merge candidates across CTAs before writing global temporary lists.
ideas.first_experiment: CDNA3-only few-row huge-vocab prototype versus non-cluster multi-CTA merge.
failure_modes: DGFX barriers cost more than global writes

ideas.name: cp.async score conveyor
ideas.core_hypothesis: Stage score tiles with async copy while candidate merge consumes the previous tile.
ideas.first_experiment: CDNA2/RDNA2/CDNA3 cp.async prototype for k=128,256,512 with amdclang++ resource/ISA/AMD GCN ISA checks.
failure_modes: no reuse so shared staging just adds traffic
compiler does not emit intended async path

ideas.name: Token-class split lanes
ideas.core_hypothesis: Split vocabulary into token classes, run class-local Top-M, and repair with a global merge.
ideas.first_experiment: Special/control/numeric/frequent/rare class split with real trace hit-rate measurement.
failure_modes: global repair always needs full scan

ideas.name: Gumbel race sampler
ideas.core_hypothesis: Use argmax over logit plus Gumbel noise or Gumbel candidate races when the caller needs samples rather than materialized Top-K.
ideas.first_experiment: Distribution-test Gumbel paths against exact categorical and Top-K-filtered sampling.
failure_modes: statistical tests replace exact token equality

ideas.name: Sparsemax/simplex decoder
ideas.core_hypothesis: Unify Top-K, Top-P, min-p, and sparse projection-style decoding as thresholded simplex operations.
ideas.first_experiment: Implement one threshold solver and evaluate latency plus quality metrics separately from exact Top-K.

ideas.name: Draft-token candidate reuse
ideas.core_hypothesis: Use draft-model tokens and accepted spans as hints for verifier-model candidate regions, then repair exactly.
ideas.first_experiment: Trace accepted/rejected span hit rates and compare verifier Top-K with and without hints.

ideas.name: Model-trace offline tuner
ideas.core_hypothesis: Mine real logits traces to choose dispatch rules, pivots, bucket sizes, and eligibility for wild ideas.
ideas.first_experiment: Use train/holdout trace splits to select pivot and bucket heuristics without overfitting reported results.

ideas.name: Split-bucket one-iteration kernel
ideas.core_hypothesis: Fuse bucket partitioning, local selection, and merge-ready candidate emission into one execution step while data is hot.
ideas.first_experiment: Implement one SBP-style iteration for k=128,256,512,1024 and compare to separate bucket/select kernels plus hipCUB radix sort.

ideas.name: Cross-layer Top-K index reuse
ideas.core_hypothesis: Reuse Top-K sparse-attention indices from anchor layers in nearby layers, or validate and repair them when exactness is required.
ideas.first_experiment: Measure anchor/reuse interval, reuse hit rate, quality drift, and repair cost on sparse attention traces.
failure_modes: quality drift appears on reasoning workloads

first_lab_batch: Add result metadata fields: idea_id, contract_class, k_regime, repair_rate.
Implement unsorted-first plus late-sort for k=16,32.
Implement complement Top-K for high k/n.
Add low-precision prefilter with exact FP32 repair.
Add adversarial row generation before timing runs.
Add one trace-driven experiment if real logits traces are available.
